title: Measure relevance against judged queries
description: Tune ranking using a labeled set of queries with graded relevance and a metric like nDCG, not by inspecting one query's results.
body: Use when changing ranking, boosts, or analyzers. Do not tune by eyeballing a favorite query — a boost that fixes it usually regresses others you didn't check. Actions: build a judgment set (queries with graded-relevant docs), compute nDCG/precision@k/recall before and after each change, and only keep changes that improve the aggregate. Validation signals include an offline relevance metric that improves across the judgment set, not a single anecdote. Risk boundary: relevance changes tuned on one query routinely regress the broad query distribution; require a judgment-set evaluation before shipping a ranking change.
